title: podman attach and systemd no interactive shell is available
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I was wondering if there is any chance to get interactive prompt available upon container execution. Otherwise it seems that everything works fine but I have to use 'CTRL+p+q' in order to detach myself as soon as systemd finished with initialization. It will be very nice to be able to interact with shell inside container. Something like "/usr/bin/sh -l"

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Compose Dockerfile:
FROM fedora:29
RUN dnf -y clean all; dnf -y install httpd; dnf -y clean all; systemctl enable httpd
STOPSIGNAL  SIGRTMIN+3
CMD ["/usr/sbin/init"]
LABEL foo=bar

2. Build the image from Dockerfile:
sudo podman build -f Dockerfile -t httpd_image:1.0

3. Create and run container from the image:
sudo podman run --name fedora29_httpd  --privileged --interactive --tty --publish-all --env container=podman --systemd --entrypoint=/usr/sbin/init --network=host -p 8080:80 --stop-signal=RTMIN+3 httpd_image:1.0

**Describe the results you received:**
When systemd finished initializing it I don't get into shell. Last line of systemd output looks like:

[  OK  ] Started Update UTMP about System Runlevel Changes.

**Describe the results you expected:**
I was hoping to get into shell. Is it expected/possible at all? It seems that I can get shell prompt on non-systemd containers fine, e.g. like "/usr/bin/sh -l"


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
Version:       1.0.0
Go Version:    go1.11.4
Git Commit:    "49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d"
Built:         Mon Jan 14 21:38:17 2019
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: I think you need to enable a getty in systemd inside the container - unlike on interactive installations, systemd in a container defaults to not enabling the relevant services (not usually needed - if you need a shell inside the container, you'd usually `podman exec -t -i <id> /bin/bash`)
Would podman exec be an option?  But if @mheon suggestion works for you that would be good.
Thank you @mheon  and @rhatdan ! Indeed this does exactly what I needed!
